comment_text.sn2.21:1.3: This is the only clear appearance of Shiva (siva, “auspicious”, “kindly”) in early Pali (but see Sivaka at SN 10.8). In the Rig Veda he appears a few times under the name Rudra, the “roarer”, where he is feared but approached as dispenser of blessed healing (Rig Veda 2.33). Along with other gods, he is there sometimes described as śiva, “kindly, blessed”. As time went on his kindly aspect became more prominent, but he never lost his destructive side. He was not normally known as Shiva until the Purāṇic period. | The next few deities have names that are similar in meaning: khema, serī.

root_text.sn2.21:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.sn2.21:1.3: Atha kho sivo devaputto abhikkantāya rattiyā abhikkantavaṇṇo kevalakappaṁ jetavanaṁ obhāsetvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi.
root_text.sn2.21:1.4: Ekamantaṁ ṭhito kho sivo devaputto bhagavato santike imā gāthāyo abhāsi:
root_text.sn2.21:8.1: Atha kho bhagavā sivaṁ devaputtaṁ gāthāya paccabhāsi:

translation_text.sn2.21:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.sn2.21:1.3: Then, late at night, the glorious godling Shiva, lighting up the entire Jeta’s Grove, went up to the Buddha, bowed, stood to one side,
translation_text.sn2.21:1.4: and recited these verses in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn2.21:2.2: Try to get close to the virtuous!
translation_text.sn2.21:2.3: Understanding the true teaching of the good,
translation_text.sn2.21:3.2: Try to get close to the virtuous!
translation_text.sn2.21:3.3: Understanding the true teaching of the good,
translation_text.sn2.21:3.4: wisdom is gained—but not from anyone else.
translation_text.sn2.21:4.2: Try to get close to the virtuous!
translation_text.sn2.21:4.3: Understanding the true teaching of the good,
translation_text.sn2.21:4.4: you grieve not among the grieving.
translation_text.sn2.21:5.2: Try to get close to the virtuous!
translation_text.sn2.21:5.3: Understanding the true teaching of the good,
translation_text.sn2.21:6.2: Try to get close to the virtuous!
translation_text.sn2.21:6.3: Understanding the true teaching of the good,
translation_text.sn2.21:6.4: sentient beings go to a good place.
translation_text.sn2.21:7.2: Try to get close to the virtuous!
translation_text.sn2.21:7.3: Understanding the true teaching of the good,
translation_text.sn2.21:8.1: Then the Buddha replied to Shiva in verse:
translation_text.sn2.21:9.2: Try to get close to the virtuous!
translation_text.sn2.21:9.3: Understanding the true teaching of the good,
